beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, check email on phone
beats.reasoning: Tyler is a disciplined workaholic who wakes early; 30 min is enough for his morning routine before heading to the office.
SLU to Amazon HQ is walkable/bikeable (~1 km); Tyler is in by 7:30 as per his habit, arriving by 7:50 to settle in.

beats.activity: Deep work on SDE3 projects, code review, standup
beats.reasoning: Intense morning focus block; Tyler is heads-down on engineering work until mid-morning, missing the 11am FIFA match (not relevant to a workaholic SDE3).

beats.activity: Eat lunch at desk: meal-prepped container from Sunday
beats.reasoning: Tyler eats lunch at his desk per routine; USA–Wales match airs now but he stays focused on work, not a sports fan.
Long afternoon block of high-focus work, typical for an intense SDE3; Light Rail closure between Westlake and U-District doesn't affect him (he walks/bikes).
Tyler hits the gym at 6 PM sharp per his regimen; short commute from Amazon HQ to a central gym location.

beats.activity: Weight training and cardio: legs + 20 min row
beats.reasoning: Disciplined 90-min workout tracking calories and reps in his spreadsheet; consistent with his intensity and tracking habits.
Travel back to SLU after gym; enough time to account for shower at gym and walk/transit home.

beats.activity: Dinner prep: grill chicken, roast vegetables for tomorrow
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down with meal prep; Tyler is methodical and prepares for the week even mid-week, consistent with spreadsheet tracking.

beats.activity: Eat dinner, review workout log and daily metrics in spreadsheet
beats.reasoning: Quiet evening at home; Tyler reviews his tracking spreadsheets, logs gym data, and unwinds before bed.
8-hour sleep cycle; Tyler is disciplined about rest to maintain high performance at work the next day.

diary:
Monday flew by in that familiar rhythm. Woke up early, grabbed coffee while scanning emails—nothing urgent, which felt like a win. The walk to HQ was crisp, and I settled into deep work pretty quickly. Code reviews took longer than expected, but that's fine; someone caught a potential race condition I would've missed.

Skipped the World Cup match everyone was talking about. Honestly, I'd rather stick to my routine than get caught up in the office buzz around USA-Wales. Grabbed my meal-prepped container at my desk around 10 and kept moving through the afternoon.

Gym session was solid. Legs always leave me feeling wrung out, but the rowing machine felt smooth today. The leg work hit different. By the time I got home, I had just enough energy to grill chicken and roast vegetables for the week. Reviewed my metrics while eating—nothing surprising, but the consistency feels good. Ready to do it all again tomorrow.
thought_process: I kept my head down today, which felt right. The World Cup buzz was everywhere at work, but I'm not really caught up in it—sports are background noise for me. The light rail closure between Westlake and University District didn't affect me since I'm close enough to walk or bike to HQ anyway. I made deliberate choices: skipped lunch socializing, went straight to the gym instead of lingering at the office, came home to prep meals rather than grab takeout. The sunny weather helped; it always makes the commute feel less like a chore. I'm tracking my workouts obsessively these days—it's becoming a grounding ritual. Work was steady, nothing dramatic. By evening I was tired but satisfied, which is the goal.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
